articles.title: Sustainable Catalyst Workbench: Getting Started
articles.summary: Start using Sustainable Catalyst Workbench with a controlled example.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Sustainable Catalyst Workbench · Getting Started

Unit-aware calculation, symbolic math, graphing, engineering notes, domain calculators, exports, and prototyping.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 5.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide provides a safe first run of Sustainable Catalyst Workbench. It explains the product role, the main feature surface, the synthetic demonstration dataset, how to recognize a valid result, and when human or expert review is required.

Capability overview

  • Chalkboard Translator
  • Symbolic Math
  • Unit-Aware Calculation
  • Graph Studio
  • Engineering Mode
  • Domain Calculators
  • Parameter Sliders
  • Calculation Notes
  • Export Studio
  • Embedded Calculators
  • Prototyping Runners
  • Hardware and Simulation Handoffs

Prerequisites

  • A modern supported browser or runtime
  • The current installed product and service version
  • A new demonstration workspace
  • No confidential, personal, credential, or production information
  • A named reviewer for consequential output

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable tablesynthetic-demo
2Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form checksynthetic-demo
3Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJsynthetic-demo
4Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rowssynthetic-demo
5Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautionssynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 12 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Open the product from its canonical Sustainable Catalyst page or administrative route.
  2. Confirm the product and backend version before creating data.
  3. Create a synthetic workspace named WORKBENCH-RETROFIT-001.
  4. Download the CSV and JSON examples supplied below.
  5. Use the feature matrix to complete the first six feature checkpoints.
  6. Save, close, and reopen the workspace to verify persistence.
  7. Inspect warnings, sources, units, permissions, and lifecycle state.
  8. Export or hand off only after completing the verification checklist.

1. Chalkboard Translator

Action: translate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptions.

Expected result: a reviewable equation and variable table.

2. Symbolic Math

Action: differentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the result.

Expected result: a symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form check.

3. Unit-Aware Calculation

Action: convert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ.

Expected result: 450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJ.

4. Graph Studio

Action: plot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity use.

Expected result: a labeled chart tied to the source rows.

5. Engineering Mode

Action: estimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariff.

Expected result: an engineering-style note with cautions.

6. Domain Calculators

Action: run a heat-loss calculation for a community-center wall.

Expected result: a domain result with inputs, units, and method limits.

Expected output

  • a reviewable equation and variable table
  • a symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form check
  • 450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJ
  • a labeled chart tied to the source rows
  • an engineering-style note with cautions
  • a domain result with inputs, units, and method limits
  • A saved record that reopens without data loss
  • An export or handoff that identifies product, version, artifact, source, and review state

Verification

  • Every sample value remains attached to its identifier and classification.
  • Warnings remain visible and are not silently treated as approval.
  • The saved workspace reopens in a clean session.
  • The product and version shown in output match the installed release.
  • Another reviewer can understand how the output was produced.

Responsible use

Outputs support analysis and learning; consequential engineering or safety decisions require validated data, applicable standards, and qualified review.

articles.title: Sustainable Catalyst Workbench: Installation and Configuration
articles.summary: Install, configure, validate, and recover Sustainable Catalyst Workbench safely.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Sustainable Catalyst Workbench · How-to Guide

Unit-aware calculation, symbolic math, graphing, engineering notes, domain calculators, exports, and prototyping.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 5.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide covers a reversible installation or update of Sustainable Catalyst Workbench, including baseline capture, package identity, dependencies, routes, permissions, service health, sample validation, rollback, and documentation handoff.

Prerequisites

  • A verified backup
  • Administrative or repository access
  • The canonical release archive and checksum
  • A documented rollback path
  • A test workspace and the synthetic sample below

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable tablesynthetic-demo
2Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form checksynthetic-demo
3Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJsynthetic-demo
4Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rowssynthetic-demo
5Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautionssynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 12 feature rows.

Procedure

1. Capture the baseline

  1. Record the installed interface, backend, package, and schema versions.
  2. Export or back up product-owned state.
  3. Record canonical routes, shortcodes, service URLs, repository branch, and environment variables.
  4. Check for incomplete migrations or uncommitted changes.

2. Install or update

  1. Verify the archive root, manifest, checksum, and version identity.
  2. Install through the product-supported WordPress, package, or service path.
  3. Run syntax, structure, contract, and product tests before replacing the working release.
  4. Apply database or schema migrations only after the backup is confirmed.
  5. Start or refresh the backend and verify its health response.

3. Configure

  1. Set documented endpoints and secret references; never store secret values in public code.
  2. Assign least-privilege roles and product ownership.
  3. Confirm product, version, component, issue, release, collection, and article-type context where supported.
  4. Clear only the caches affected by changed assets or routes.

4. Validate

  1. Import the feature-example CSV or JSON.
  2. Test every feature row in a nonproduction workspace.
  3. Compare actual results with the expected_result field.
  4. Reopen state after a clean reload.
  5. Record differences as draft support issues before enabling real use.

Expected output

  • One consistent product version across interface, service, manifest, package, and export
  • Healthy routes and dependencies
  • A complete synthetic feature test
  • A verified backup and rollback point
  • No credentials or private data in public artifacts

Verification

  • PHP, Python, R, JavaScript, or package checks pass where applicable.
  • The product loads in an incognito or clean session.
  • The sample data can be created, saved, reopened, exported, and removed.
  • Permissions prevent unauthorized administrative or private access.
  • The previous release can be restored.

Responsible use

Outputs support analysis and learning; consequential engineering or safety decisions require validated data, applicable standards, and qualified review.

Do not delete backups or the prior working release until the installed version and all sample features have been validated.

articles.title: Sustainable Catalyst Workbench: End-to-End Sample Workflow
articles.summary: Complete an all-feature retrofit demonstration in Sustainable Catalyst Workbench.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Sustainable Catalyst Workbench · How-to Guide

Unit-aware calculation, symbolic math, graphing, engineering notes, domain calculators, exports, and prototyping.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 5.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This end-to-end workflow demonstrates every documented Sustainable Catalyst Workbench capability through one fictional resilient-community-retrofit case. The objective is not to prove a real-world conclusion; it is to verify the complete product workflow with controlled data.

Prerequisites

  • The current product release
  • A new synthetic workspace
  • The all-feature CSV and JSON
  • No production or private information
  • Time to review every checkpoint

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable tablesynthetic-demo
2Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form checksynthetic-demo
3Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJsynthetic-demo
4Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rowssynthetic-demo
5Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautionssynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 12 feature rows.

Procedure

Scenario

A community center is assessing energy, comfort, financing, implementation, evidence, and monitoring needs. Use only the supplied fictional records.

  1. 1. Chalkboard Translator: translate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptions. Confirm a reviewable equation and variable table.
  2. 2. Symbolic Math: differentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the result. Confirm a symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form check.
  3. 3. Unit-Aware Calculation: convert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ. Confirm 450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJ.
  4. 4. Graph Studio: plot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity use. Confirm a labeled chart tied to the source rows.
  5. 5. Engineering Mode: estimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariff. Confirm an engineering-style note with cautions.
  6. 6. Domain Calculators: run a heat-loss calculation for a community-center wall. Confirm a domain result with inputs, units, and method limits.
  7. 7. Parameter Sliders: vary electricity price from $0.08 to $0.18/kWh. Confirm a visible sensitivity range.
  8. 8. Calculation Notes: attach tariff source, occupancy assumption, and uncertainty. Confirm a traceable calculation note.
  9. 9. Export Studio: export the reviewed calculation as JSON, CSV, Markdown, or HTML. Confirm a portable bundle preserving inputs and provenance.
  10. 10. Embedded Calculators: place the energy calculator inside a methodology page. Confirm a reusable governed calculator.
  11. 11. Prototyping Runners: generate a bounded Python example from the reviewed model. Confirm inspectable code and execution notes.
  12. 12. Hardware and Simulation Handoffs: send sensor conversion parameters to a Research Lab experiment. Confirm a typed handoff receipt.

Save and review

  1. Save the final workspace under the sample workspace ID.
  2. Close and reopen it in a clean session.
  3. Compare every feature result with the sample expected_result field.
  4. Document unresolved differences and do not hide warnings.
  5. Export one complete artifact bundle.
  6. Send one supported handoff to an integration and verify its receipt.

Supported integrations

  • Decision Studio
  • Research Lab
  • Catalyst Data
  • Catalyst Analytics R
  • Knowledge Library
  • Platform Core

Expected output

  • a reviewable equation and variable table
  • a symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form check
  • 450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJ
  • a labeled chart tied to the source rows
  • an engineering-style note with cautions
  • a domain result with inputs, units, and method limits
  • a visible sensitivity range
  • a traceable calculation note
  • a portable bundle preserving inputs and provenance
  • a reusable governed calculator
  • inspectable code and execution notes
  • a typed handoff receipt
  • A complete saved workspace
  • A portable export
  • A validated cross-product handoff receipt

Verification

  • Every CSV feature row has a corresponding saved result.
  • The JSON workspace ID and product version are preserved.
  • The export can be inspected without temporary browser state.
  • The receiving product identifies source, destination, schema, artifact, and review state.
  • A human reviewer can reproduce or audit the demonstration.

Responsible use

Outputs support analysis and learning; consequential engineering or safety decisions require validated data, applicable standards, and qualified review.

Replace synthetic data only with information you are authorized to use, after reassessing licensing, privacy, uncertainty, and review obligations.

articles.title: Sustainable Catalyst Workbench: Troubleshooting and Recovery
articles.summary: Diagnose and recover common Sustainable Catalyst Workbench failures without losing state.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Sustainable Catalyst Workbench · Troubleshooting

Unit-aware calculation, symbolic math, graphing, engineering notes, domain calculators, exports, and prototyping.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 5.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide uses an evidence-first diagnostic sequence for Sustainable Catalyst Workbench. Preserve state before changing configuration. Determine whether the failure comes from input data, permissions, version identity, browser assets, backend health, schema compatibility, external dependencies, or a product defect.

Prerequisites

  • Exact error message and time
  • Product, backend, package, and schema versions
  • Affected route, feature, record ID, and user role
  • A safe backup or export
  • The synthetic sample for a reference test

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable tablesynthetic-demo
2Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form checksynthetic-demo
3Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJsynthetic-demo
4Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rowssynthetic-demo
5Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautionssynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 12 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Stop repeated imports, submissions, migrations, or destructive retries.
  2. Record URL, feature, action, role, versions, browser/runtime, and expected result.
  3. Run the same feature row in a new synthetic workspace.
  4. If the sample fails, inspect health, logs, browser console, network response, and validation details.
  5. If the sample succeeds, compare real input schema, units, permissions, size, source, and status with the sample.
  6. Check version and schema identity before clearing caches or reinstalling.
  7. Apply the smallest reversible change.
  8. Repeat the sample and real workflow, then document the result.

Common problems

SymptomLikely causeSafe response
Unit mismatchincompatible dimensions were combinedcorrect units or reformulate the equation
Blank graphselected fields are nonnumeric or missingselect numeric fields and document missingness
Unexpected resultsign, scale, formula, or unit is wronginspect the equation and run a known reference case
Export blockedvalidation warnings remain unresolvedresolve or document warnings before export

Escalation package

  • Product and version
  • Feature and component
  • Exact error text
  • Reproduction steps using synthetic data
  • Expected and actual result
  • Redacted log excerpt without secrets or personal data
  • Whether a clean sample reproduces the problem

Expected output

  • A reproducible diagnosis or bounded unresolved issue
  • Preserved user and system state
  • A documented workaround when available
  • A support-ready issue summary without private data

Verification

  • The synthetic feature succeeds after the fix.
  • The original workflow succeeds or remains safely isolated.
  • Version and health identity are consistent.
  • No duplicates, stale locks, or unintended cache artifacts remain.
  • The resolution is reflected in a support article, known issue, or release record when appropriate.

Responsible use

Outputs support analysis and learning; consequential engineering or safety decisions require validated data, applicable standards, and qualified review.

Never place credentials, API keys, private URLs, personal records, client documents, or confidential logs in a public article or issue.

articles.title: Sustainable Catalyst Workbench: Technical Reference and Integrations
articles.summary: Review the technical, data, lifecycle, validation, and integration contract for Sustainable Catalyst Workbench.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators

articles.content:
Sustainable Catalyst Workbench · Technical Reference

Unit-aware calculation, symbolic math, graphing, engineering notes, domain calculators, exports, and prototyping.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 5.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This reference summarizes the functional contract, record expectations, lifecycle, validation, export, compatibility, integration, privacy, and release-review requirements for Sustainable Catalyst Workbench.

Prerequisites

  • Current release notes and repository documentation
  • A test environment
  • A backup and rollback plan
  • Access to product health or package checks
  • The synthetic all-feature sample

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable tablesynthetic-demo
2Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form checksynthetic-demo
3Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJsynthetic-demo
4Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rowssynthetic-demo
5Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautionssynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 12 feature rows.

Technical contract

Canonical identity

FieldValue
Product slugworkbench
Product nameSustainable Catalyst Workbench
Verified version5.0.0
Content pack1.0.0
Default article statedraft
Human reviewrequired

Functional surface

CapabilitySample operationExpected artifact
Chalkboard Translatortranslate a plain-language energy question into variables, equations, units, and assumptionsa reviewable equation and variable table
Symbolic Mathdifferentiate P(t)=120+8t-0.2t² and simplify the resulta symbolic derivative with an equivalent-form check
Unit-Aware Calculationconvert 450 MWh to kWh and GJ450,000 kWh and about 1,620 GJ
Graph Studioplot monthly baseline and retrofit electricity usea labeled chart tied to the source rows
Engineering Modeestimate simple payback with stated capital cost and tariffan engineering-style note with cautions
Domain Calculatorsrun a heat-loss calculation for a community-center walla domain result with inputs, units, and method limits
Parameter Slidersvary electricity price from $0.08 to $0.18/kWha visible sensitivity range
Calculation Notesattach tariff source, occupancy assumption, and uncertaintya traceable calculation note
Export Studioexport the reviewed calculation as JSON, CSV, Markdown, or HTMLa portable bundle preserving inputs and provenance
Embedded Calculatorsplace the energy calculator inside a methodology pagea reusable governed calculator
Prototyping Runnersgenerate a bounded Python example from the reviewed modelinspectable code and execution notes
Hardware and Simulation Handoffssend sensor conversion parameters to a Research Lab experimenta typed handoff receipt

Core record fields

  • Stable record or artifact ID
  • Product and schema version
  • Created and updated time
  • Owner or responsible role
  • Lifecycle or review state
  • Source and provenance
  • Units, period, geography, classification, or audience where applicable
  • Validation findings and uncertainty
  • Privacy and publication boundary
  • Export or handoff integrity metadata

Lifecycle

Use a human-controlled lifecycle such as draft → review → approved/published, with revise, superseded, archived, withdrawn, failed, and recovered states where appropriate. Automated analysis may recommend; it must not silently approve, publish, or create private cases.

Validation

  • Required-field and type checks
  • Identifier and relationship checks
  • Unit, period, geography, method, and classification compatibility
  • Product and schema compatibility
  • Source, evidence, and provenance completeness
  • Permission, privacy, and publication-boundary checks
  • Deterministic checksum or fingerprint validation for portable artifacts

Integrations

  • Decision Studio
  • Research Lab
  • Catalyst Data
  • Catalyst Analytics R
  • Knowledge Library
  • Platform Core

Send the minimum required fields through a typed contract. Preserve source product, destination, schema, artifact ID, review state, provenance, consent or privacy boundary, and receipt. Private Contact and Engagement records never belong in public support or research artifacts.

Release review

  1. Confirm interface, service, manifest, package, and schema versions.
  2. Validate the all-feature sample.
  3. Run product tests and integrity checks.
  4. Test one export and each supported handoff category.
  5. Document breaking changes and migration path.
  6. Update Last Verified Version before publishing this article.

Expected output

  • A complete feature contract
  • A validated synthetic export
  • A compatibility decision
  • A documented integration receipt
  • A release-integrity report

Verification

  • Product slug, version, schema, and IDs agree.
  • Every documented feature maps to an actual interface, command, route, or record.
  • The sample files produce the stated artifacts.
  • Integrations preserve provenance and privacy.
  • Migration, rollback, support, and release documentation are current.

Responsible use

Outputs support analysis and learning; consequential engineering or safety decisions require validated data, applicable standards, and qualified review.

articles.title: Decision Studio: Getting Started
articles.summary: Start using Decision Studio with a controlled example.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Decision Studio · Getting Started

Auditable decision packets, evidence, scenarios, tradeoffs, risk, briefing, publication, monitoring, and reassessment.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 2.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide provides a safe first run of Decision Studio. It explains the product role, the main feature surface, the synthetic demonstration dataset, how to recognize a valid result, and when human or expert review is required.

Capability overview

  • Decision Packet
  • Problem Framing
  • Evidence Ledger
  • Scenario Studio
  • Impact Records
  • Claim Review
  • Finance Tradeoffs
  • Risk and Recovery
  • Assumption Register
  • Workbench Handoffs
  • Readiness Review
  • Briefing Studio
  • Publication Studio
  • Outcomes Monitoring
  • Reassessment
  • API and Embeds

Prerequisites

  • A modern supported browser or runtime
  • The current installed product and service version
  • A new demonstration workspace
  • No confidential, personal, credential, or production information
  • A named reviewer for consequential output

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifactssynthetic-demo
2Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statementsynthetic-demo
3Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence recordssynthetic-demo
4Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measuressynthetic-demo
5Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entriessynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Open the product from its canonical Sustainable Catalyst page or administrative route.
  2. Confirm the product and backend version before creating data.
  3. Create a synthetic workspace named DECISION-STUDIO-RETROFIT-001.
  4. Download the CSV and JSON examples supplied below.
  5. Use the feature matrix to complete the first six feature checkpoints.
  6. Save, close, and reopen the workspace to verify persistence.
  7. Inspect warnings, sources, units, permissions, and lifecycle state.
  8. Export or hand off only after completing the verification checklist.

1. Decision Packet

Action: create DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choice.

Expected result: a versioned packet containing all decision artifacts.

2. Problem Framing

Action: define the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadline.

Expected result: a bounded decision statement.

3. Evidence Ledger

Action: add utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidence.

Expected result: traceable evidence records.

4. Scenario Studio

Action: compare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofit.

Expected result: a scenario table with aligned measures.

5. Impact Records

Action: record cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effects.

Expected result: structured impact entries.

6. Claim Review

Action: review the claim that the project pays back in five years.

Expected result: a supported, contested, or unresolved claim state.

Expected output

  • a versioned packet containing all decision artifacts
  • a bounded decision statement
  • traceable evidence records
  • a scenario table with aligned measures
  • structured impact entries
  • a supported, contested, or unresolved claim state
  • A saved record that reopens without data loss
  • An export or handoff that identifies product, version, artifact, source, and review state

Verification

  • Every sample value remains attached to its identifier and classification.
  • Warnings remain visible and are not silently treated as approval.
  • The saved workspace reopens in a clean session.
  • The product and version shown in output match the installed release.
  • Another reviewer can understand how the output was produced.

Responsible use

The product organizes evidence and reasoning; accountable decision owners, due process, experts, and institutional approvals remain necessary.

articles.title: Decision Studio: Installation and Configuration

articles.content:
Decision Studio · How-to Guide

Auditable decision packets, evidence, scenarios, tradeoffs, risk, briefing, publication, monitoring, and reassessment.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 2.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide covers a reversible installation or update of Decision Studio, including baseline capture, package identity, dependencies, routes, permissions, service health, sample validation, rollback, and documentation handoff.

Prerequisites

  • A verified backup
  • Administrative or repository access
  • The canonical release archive and checksum
  • A documented rollback path
  • A test workspace and the synthetic sample below

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifactssynthetic-demo
2Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statementsynthetic-demo
3Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence recordssynthetic-demo
4Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measuressynthetic-demo
5Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entriessynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

1. Capture the baseline

  1. Record the installed interface, backend, package, and schema versions.
  2. Export or back up product-owned state.
  3. Record canonical routes, shortcodes, service URLs, repository branch, and environment variables.
  4. Check for incomplete migrations or uncommitted changes.

2. Install or update

  1. Verify the archive root, manifest, checksum, and version identity.
  2. Install through the product-supported WordPress, package, or service path.
  3. Run syntax, structure, contract, and product tests before replacing the working release.
  4. Apply database or schema migrations only after the backup is confirmed.
  5. Start or refresh the backend and verify its health response.

3. Configure

  1. Set documented endpoints and secret references; never store secret values in public code.
  2. Assign least-privilege roles and product ownership.
  3. Confirm product, version, component, issue, release, collection, and article-type context where supported.
  4. Clear only the caches affected by changed assets or routes.

4. Validate

  1. Import the feature-example CSV or JSON.
  2. Test every feature row in a nonproduction workspace.
  3. Compare actual results with the expected_result field.
  4. Reopen state after a clean reload.
  5. Record differences as draft support issues before enabling real use.

Expected output

  • One consistent product version across interface, service, manifest, package, and export
  • Healthy routes and dependencies
  • A complete synthetic feature test
  • A verified backup and rollback point
  • No credentials or private data in public artifacts

Verification

  • PHP, Python, R, JavaScript, or package checks pass where applicable.
  • The product loads in an incognito or clean session.
  • The sample data can be created, saved, reopened, exported, and removed.
  • Permissions prevent unauthorized administrative or private access.
  • The previous release can be restored.

Responsible use

The product organizes evidence and reasoning; accountable decision owners, due process, experts, and institutional approvals remain necessary.

Do not delete backups or the prior working release until the installed version and all sample features have been validated.

Decision Studio · How-to Guide

Auditable decision packets, evidence, scenarios, tradeoffs, risk, briefing, publication, monitoring, and reassessment.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 2.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This end-to-end workflow demonstrates every documented Decision Studio capability through one fictional resilient-community-retrofit case. The objective is not to prove a real-world conclusion; it is to verify the complete product workflow with controlled data.

Prerequisites

  • The current product release
  • A new synthetic workspace
  • The all-feature CSV and JSON
  • No production or private information
  • Time to review every checkpoint

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifactssynthetic-demo
2Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statementsynthetic-demo
3Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence recordssynthetic-demo
4Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measuressynthetic-demo
5Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entriessynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

Scenario

A community center is assessing energy, comfort, financing, implementation, evidence, and monitoring needs. Use only the supplied fictional records.

  1. 1. Decision Packet: create DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choice. Confirm a versioned packet containing all decision artifacts.
  2. 2. Problem Framing: define the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadline. Confirm a bounded decision statement.
  3. 3. Evidence Ledger: add utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidence. Confirm traceable evidence records.
  4. 4. Scenario Studio: compare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofit. Confirm a scenario table with aligned measures.
  5. 5. Impact Records: record cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effects. Confirm structured impact entries.
  6. 6. Claim Review: review the claim that the project pays back in five years. Confirm a supported, contested, or unresolved claim state.
  7. 7. Finance Tradeoffs: compare capital, operating, lifecycle, and uncertainty considerations. Confirm a transparent tradeoff record.
  8. 8. Risk and Recovery: record contractor delay, budget overrun, rollback, and recovery. Confirm a risk register with owners and mitigations.
  9. 9. Assumption Register: record 3% annual tariff growth with source and confidence. Confirm a versioned assumption.
  10. 10. Workbench Handoffs: request lifecycle-cost and emissions calculations. Confirm a returned calculation artifact and receipt.
  11. 11. Readiness Review: check evidence, privacy, unresolved issues, and approval requirements. Confirm an advisory readiness report.
  12. 12. Briefing Studio: assemble a board briefing from reviewed packet sections. Confirm an executive brief.
  13. 13. Publication Studio: prepare a redacted public decision summary. Confirm a controlled publication artifact.
  14. 14. Outcomes Monitoring: track six months of post-retrofit energy and comfort data. Confirm outcome records and variance explanations.
  15. 15. Reassessment: reopen the decision after grant or outcome changes. Confirm a new review cycle preserving history.
  16. 16. API and Embeds: expose an approved public packet summary. Confirm a scoped privacy-aware JSON or embed.

Save and review

  1. Save the final workspace under the sample workspace ID.
  2. Close and reopen it in a clean session.
  3. Compare every feature result with the sample expected_result field.
  4. Document unresolved differences and do not hide warnings.
  5. Export one complete artifact bundle.
  6. Send one supported handoff to an integration and verify its receipt.

Supported integrations

  • Workbench
  • Catalyst Finance
  • Catalyst Data
  • Site Intelligence
  • Knowledge Library
  • Narrative Risk
  • Platform Core

Expected output

  • a versioned packet containing all decision artifacts
  • a bounded decision statement
  • traceable evidence records
  • a scenario table with aligned measures
  • structured impact entries
  • a supported, contested, or unresolved claim state
  • a transparent tradeoff record
  • a risk register with owners and mitigations
  • a versioned assumption
  • a returned calculation artifact and receipt
  • an advisory readiness report
  • an executive brief
  • a controlled publication artifact
  • outcome records and variance explanations
  • a new review cycle preserving history
  • a scoped privacy-aware JSON or embed
  • A complete saved workspace
  • A portable export
  • A validated cross-product handoff receipt

Verification

  • Every CSV feature row has a corresponding saved result.
  • The JSON workspace ID and product version are preserved.
  • The export can be inspected without temporary browser state.
  • The receiving product identifies source, destination, schema, artifact, and review state.
  • A human reviewer can reproduce or audit the demonstration.

Responsible use

The product organizes evidence and reasoning; accountable decision owners, due process, experts, and institutional approvals remain necessary.

Replace synthetic data only with information you are authorized to use, after reassessing licensing, privacy, uncertainty, and review obligations.

articles.title: Decision Studio: Troubleshooting and Recovery
articles.summary: Diagnose and recover common Decision Studio failures without losing state.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Decision Studio · Troubleshooting

Auditable decision packets, evidence, scenarios, tradeoffs, risk, briefing, publication, monitoring, and reassessment.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 2.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide uses an evidence-first diagnostic sequence for Decision Studio. Preserve state before changing configuration. Determine whether the failure comes from input data, permissions, version identity, browser assets, backend health, schema compatibility, external dependencies, or a product defect.

Prerequisites

  • Exact error message and time
  • Product, backend, package, and schema versions
  • Affected route, feature, record ID, and user role
  • A safe backup or export
  • The synthetic sample for a reference test

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifactssynthetic-demo
2Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statementsynthetic-demo
3Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence recordssynthetic-demo
4Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measuressynthetic-demo
5Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entriessynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Stop repeated imports, submissions, migrations, or destructive retries.
  2. Record URL, feature, action, role, versions, browser/runtime, and expected result.
  3. Run the same feature row in a new synthetic workspace.
  4. If the sample fails, inspect health, logs, browser console, network response, and validation details.
  5. If the sample succeeds, compare real input schema, units, permissions, size, source, and status with the sample.
  6. Check version and schema identity before clearing caches or reinstalling.
  7. Apply the smallest reversible change.
  8. Repeat the sample and real workflow, then document the result.

Common problems

SymptomLikely causeSafe response
Packet not readyowner, evidence, review, or boundary fields are missingresolve each readiness blocker
Options cannot be compareddefinitions, units, periods, or methods differnormalize or mark non-comparable
Handoff result missingexternal result has not returned or receipt is invalidretry safely or attach a reviewed result
Publication blockedprivate or unresolved material remainsredact, resolve, or change the boundary

Escalation package

  • Product and version
  • Feature and component
  • Exact error text
  • Reproduction steps using synthetic data
  • Expected and actual result
  • Redacted log excerpt without secrets or personal data
  • Whether a clean sample reproduces the problem

Expected output

  • A reproducible diagnosis or bounded unresolved issue
  • Preserved user and system state
  • A documented workaround when available
  • A support-ready issue summary without private data

Verification

  • The synthetic feature succeeds after the fix.
  • The original workflow succeeds or remains safely isolated.
  • Version and health identity are consistent.
  • No duplicates, stale locks, or unintended cache artifacts remain.
  • The resolution is reflected in a support article, known issue, or release record when appropriate.

Responsible use

The product organizes evidence and reasoning; accountable decision owners, due process, experts, and institutional approvals remain necessary.

Never place credentials, API keys, private URLs, personal records, client documents, or confidential logs in a public article or issue.

articles.content:
Decision Studio · Technical Reference

Auditable decision packets, evidence, scenarios, tradeoffs, risk, briefing, publication, monitoring, and reassessment.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 2.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This reference summarizes the functional contract, record expectations, lifecycle, validation, export, compatibility, integration, privacy, and release-review requirements for Decision Studio.

Prerequisites

  • Current release notes and repository documentation
  • A test environment
  • A backup and rollback plan
  • Access to product health or package checks
  • The synthetic all-feature sample

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifactssynthetic-demo
2Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statementsynthetic-demo
3Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence recordssynthetic-demo
4Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measuressynthetic-demo
5Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entriessynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Technical contract

Canonical identity

FieldValue
Product slugdecision-studio
Product nameDecision Studio
Verified version2.0.0
Content pack1.0.0
Default article statedraft
Human reviewrequired

Functional surface

CapabilitySample operationExpected artifact
Decision Packetcreate DS-RETROFIT-001 for a community-center retrofit choicea versioned packet containing all decision artifacts
Problem Framingdefine the decision, scope, objectives, constraints, stakeholders, and deadlinea bounded decision statement
Evidence Ledgeradd utility bills and an engineering assessment with relevance and confidencetraceable evidence records
Scenario Studiocompare no action, controls-only, and whole-building retrofita scenario table with aligned measures
Impact Recordsrecord cost, emissions, comfort, disruption, and equity effectsstructured impact entries
Claim Reviewreview the claim that the project pays back in five yearsa supported, contested, or unresolved claim state
Finance Tradeoffscompare capital, operating, lifecycle, and uncertainty considerationsa transparent tradeoff record
Risk and Recoveryrecord contractor delay, budget overrun, rollback, and recoverya risk register with owners and mitigations
Assumption Registerrecord 3% annual tariff growth with source and confidencea versioned assumption
Workbench Handoffsrequest lifecycle-cost and emissions calculationsa returned calculation artifact and receipt
Readiness Reviewcheck evidence, privacy, unresolved issues, and approval requirementsan advisory readiness report
Briefing Studioassemble a board briefing from reviewed packet sectionsan executive brief
Publication Studioprepare a redacted public decision summarya controlled publication artifact
Outcomes Monitoringtrack six months of post-retrofit energy and comfort dataoutcome records and variance explanations
Reassessmentreopen the decision after grant or outcome changesa new review cycle preserving history
API and Embedsexpose an approved public packet summarya scoped privacy-aware JSON or embed

Core record fields

  • Stable record or artifact ID
  • Product and schema version
  • Created and updated time
  • Owner or responsible role
  • Lifecycle or review state
  • Source and provenance
  • Units, period, geography, classification, or audience where applicable
  • Validation findings and uncertainty
  • Privacy and publication boundary
  • Export or handoff integrity metadata

Lifecycle

Use a human-controlled lifecycle such as draft → review → approved/published, with revise, superseded, archived, withdrawn, failed, and recovered states where appropriate. Automated analysis may recommend; it must not silently approve, publish, or create private cases.

Validation

  • Required-field and type checks
  • Identifier and relationship checks
  • Unit, period, geography, method, and classification compatibility
  • Product and schema compatibility
  • Source, evidence, and provenance completeness
  • Permission, privacy, and publication-boundary checks
  • Deterministic checksum or fingerprint validation for portable artifacts

Integrations

  • Workbench
  • Catalyst Finance
  • Catalyst Data
  • Site Intelligence
  • Knowledge Library
  • Narrative Risk
  • Platform Core

Send the minimum required fields through a typed contract. Preserve source product, destination, schema, artifact ID, review state, provenance, consent or privacy boundary, and receipt. Private Contact and Engagement records never belong in public support or research artifacts.

Release review

  1. Confirm interface, service, manifest, package, and schema versions.
  2. Validate the all-feature sample.
  3. Run product tests and integrity checks.
  4. Test one export and each supported handoff category.
  5. Document breaking changes and migration path.
  6. Update Last Verified Version before publishing this article.

Expected output

  • A complete feature contract
  • A validated synthetic export
  • A compatibility decision
  • A documented integration receipt
  • A release-integrity report

Verification

  • Product slug, version, schema, and IDs agree.
  • Every documented feature maps to an actual interface, command, route, or record.
  • The sample files produce the stated artifacts.
  • Integrations preserve provenance and privacy.
  • Migration, rollback, support, and release documentation are current.

Responsible use

The product organizes evidence and reasoning; accountable decision owners, due process, experts, and institutional approvals remain necessary.

articles.content:
Site Intelligence · Getting Started

Connected country, regional, earth-systems, economic, humanitarian, trade, energy, event, alert, scenario, and briefing intelligence.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 3.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide provides a safe first run of Site Intelligence. It explains the product role, the main feature surface, the synthetic demonstration dataset, how to recognize a valid result, and when human or expert review is required.

Capability overview

  • Country Dossiers
  • Regional Comparison
  • Earth Systems Observatory
  • Economics and Markets
  • Humanitarian and Conflict
  • Trade and Energy
  • Live Event Intelligence
  • Alerts and Monitoring
  • Scenario Studio
  • Research Paths
  • Saved Investigations
  • Briefing Workflows
  • Source and Provenance Panels
  • Public API
  • Embeds
  • Offline and Accessible Views

Prerequisites

  • A modern supported browser or runtime
  • The current installed product and service version
  • A new demonstration workspace
  • No confidential, personal, credential, or production information
  • A named reviewer for consequential output

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Country Dossiersopen a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadataa multi-domain country overviewsynthetic-demo
2Regional Comparisoncompare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Ugandaan aligned table or chartsynthetic-demo
3Earth Systems Observatoryreview rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signalsa time-bounded earth-systems viewsynthetic-demo
4Economics and Marketsinspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicatorsa sourced economic context viewsynthetic-demo
5Humanitarian and Conflictreview public displacement and humanitarian-need signalsa harm-aware summarysynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Open the product from its canonical Sustainable Catalyst page or administrative route.
  2. Confirm the product and backend version before creating data.
  3. Create a synthetic workspace named SITE-INTELLIGENCE-RETROFIT-001.
  4. Download the CSV and JSON examples supplied below.
  5. Use the feature matrix to complete the first six feature checkpoints.
  6. Save, close, and reopen the workspace to verify persistence.
  7. Inspect warnings, sources, units, permissions, and lifecycle state.
  8. Export or hand off only after completing the verification checklist.

1. Country Dossiers

Action: open a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadata.

Expected result: a multi-domain country overview.

2. Regional Comparison

Action: compare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda.

Expected result: an aligned table or chart.

3. Earth Systems Observatory

Action: review rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signals.

Expected result: a time-bounded earth-systems view.

4. Economics and Markets

Action: inspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicators.

Expected result: a sourced economic context view.

5. Humanitarian and Conflict

Action: review public displacement and humanitarian-need signals.

Expected result: a harm-aware summary.

6. Trade and Energy

Action: review fuel-import dependence and energy-system exposure.

Expected result: a trade and energy profile.

Expected output

  • a multi-domain country overview
  • an aligned table or chart
  • a time-bounded earth-systems view
  • a sourced economic context view
  • a harm-aware summary
  • a trade and energy profile
  • A saved record that reopens without data loss
  • An export or handoff that identifies product, version, artifact, source, and review state

Verification

  • Every sample value remains attached to its identifier and classification.
  • Warnings remain visible and are not silently treated as approval.
  • The saved workspace reopens in a clean session.
  • The product and version shown in output match the installed release.
  • Another reviewer can understand how the output was produced.

Responsible use

Public intelligence may be incomplete, delayed, or contested; do not use it for targeting, surveillance, emergency command, or unsupported prediction.

articles.content:
Site Intelligence · How-to Guide

Connected country, regional, earth-systems, economic, humanitarian, trade, energy, event, alert, scenario, and briefing intelligence.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 3.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide covers a reversible installation or update of Site Intelligence, including baseline capture, package identity, dependencies, routes, permissions, service health, sample validation, rollback, and documentation handoff.

Prerequisites

  • A verified backup
  • Administrative or repository access
  • The canonical release archive and checksum
  • A documented rollback path
  • A test workspace and the synthetic sample below

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Country Dossiersopen a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadataa multi-domain country overviewsynthetic-demo
2Regional Comparisoncompare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Ugandaan aligned table or chartsynthetic-demo
3Earth Systems Observatoryreview rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signalsa time-bounded earth-systems viewsynthetic-demo
4Economics and Marketsinspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicatorsa sourced economic context viewsynthetic-demo
5Humanitarian and Conflictreview public displacement and humanitarian-need signalsa harm-aware summarysynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

1. Capture the baseline

  1. Record the installed interface, backend, package, and schema versions.
  2. Export or back up product-owned state.
  3. Record canonical routes, shortcodes, service URLs, repository branch, and environment variables.
  4. Check for incomplete migrations or uncommitted changes.

2. Install or update

  1. Verify the archive root, manifest, checksum, and version identity.
  2. Install through the product-supported WordPress, package, or service path.
  3. Run syntax, structure, contract, and product tests before replacing the working release.
  4. Apply database or schema migrations only after the backup is confirmed.
  5. Start or refresh the backend and verify its health response.

3. Configure

  1. Set documented endpoints and secret references; never store secret values in public code.
  2. Assign least-privilege roles and product ownership.
  3. Confirm product, version, component, issue, release, collection, and article-type context where supported.
  4. Clear only the caches affected by changed assets or routes.

4. Validate

  1. Import the feature-example CSV or JSON.
  2. Test every feature row in a nonproduction workspace.
  3. Compare actual results with the expected_result field.
  4. Reopen state after a clean reload.
  5. Record differences as draft support issues before enabling real use.

Expected output

  • One consistent product version across interface, service, manifest, package, and export
  • Healthy routes and dependencies
  • A complete synthetic feature test
  • A verified backup and rollback point
  • No credentials or private data in public artifacts

Verification

  • PHP, Python, R, JavaScript, or package checks pass where applicable.
  • The product loads in an incognito or clean session.
  • The sample data can be created, saved, reopened, exported, and removed.
  • Permissions prevent unauthorized administrative or private access.
  • The previous release can be restored.

Responsible use

Public intelligence may be incomplete, delayed, or contested; do not use it for targeting, surveillance, emergency command, or unsupported prediction.

Do not delete backups or the prior working release until the installed version and all sample features have been validated.

Purpose

This is the complete documented feature inventory for Site Intelligence version 3.0.0. Every feature includes sample data, a seven-step use pattern, expected output, and a verification rule. Reverify this article whenever the product release changes.

Prerequisites

  • A clean demonstration workspace
  • The downloadable all-feature CSV or JSON
  • Current product release notes
  • Permission to use each tested feature
  • A reviewer familiar with the product

articles.title: Site Intelligence: End-to-End Sample Workflow
articles.summary: Complete an all-feature retrofit demonstration in Site Intelligence.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Site Intelligence · How-to Guide

Connected country, regional, earth-systems, economic, humanitarian, trade, energy, event, alert, scenario, and briefing intelligence.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 3.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This end-to-end workflow demonstrates every documented Site Intelligence capability through one fictional resilient-community-retrofit case. The objective is not to prove a real-world conclusion; it is to verify the complete product workflow with controlled data.

Prerequisites

  • The current product release
  • A new synthetic workspace
  • The all-feature CSV and JSON
  • No production or private information
  • Time to review every checkpoint

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Country Dossiersopen a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadataa multi-domain country overviewsynthetic-demo
2Regional Comparisoncompare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Ugandaan aligned table or chartsynthetic-demo
3Earth Systems Observatoryreview rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signalsa time-bounded earth-systems viewsynthetic-demo
4Economics and Marketsinspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicatorsa sourced economic context viewsynthetic-demo
5Humanitarian and Conflictreview public displacement and humanitarian-need signalsa harm-aware summarysynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

Scenario

A community center is assessing energy, comfort, financing, implementation, evidence, and monitoring needs. Use only the supplied fictional records.

  1. 1. Country Dossiers: open a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadata. Confirm a multi-domain country overview.
  2. 2. Regional Comparison: compare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda. Confirm an aligned table or chart.
  3. 3. Earth Systems Observatory: review rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signals. Confirm a time-bounded earth-systems view.
  4. 4. Economics and Markets: inspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicators. Confirm a sourced economic context view.
  5. 5. Humanitarian and Conflict: review public displacement and humanitarian-need signals. Confirm a harm-aware summary.
  6. 6. Trade and Energy: review fuel-import dependence and energy-system exposure. Confirm a trade and energy profile.
  7. 7. Live Event Intelligence: review severe-weather events from the last seven days. Confirm timestamped event records.
  8. 8. Alerts and Monitoring: watch a rainfall anomaly threshold. Confirm an alert candidate requiring interpretation.
  9. 9. Scenario Studio: compare drought persistence and rainfall recovery. Confirm scenario assumptions and monitored signals.
  10. 10. Research Paths: build an East Africa food-security research path. Confirm an ordered reusable investigation.
  11. 11. Saved Investigations: save filters, records, notes, and evidence. Confirm a persistent investigation workspace.
  12. 12. Briefing Workflows: create a weekly regional brief. Confirm a source-linked briefing.
  13. 13. Source and Provenance Panels: inspect publisher, retrieval, method, license, and transformation. Confirm a transparent source record.
  14. 14. Public API: request an approved country-summary response. Confirm structured rate-limited JSON.
  15. 15. Embeds: embed a public country or indicator card. Confirm a scoped component.
  16. 16. Offline and Accessible Views: save a keyboard-readable low-bandwidth briefing. Confirm a resilient degraded-mode artifact.

Save and review

  1. Save the final workspace under the sample workspace ID.
  2. Close and reopen it in a clean session.
  3. Compare every feature result with the sample expected_result field.
  4. Document unresolved differences and do not hide warnings.
  5. Export one complete artifact bundle.
  6. Send one supported handoff to an integration and verify its receipt.

Supported integrations

  • Decision Studio
  • Research Librarian
  • Knowledge Library
  • Catalyst Data
  • Research Lab
  • Platform Core

Expected output

  • a multi-domain country overview
  • an aligned table or chart
  • a time-bounded earth-systems view
  • a sourced economic context view
  • a harm-aware summary
  • a trade and energy profile
  • timestamped event records
  • an alert candidate requiring interpretation
  • scenario assumptions and monitored signals
  • an ordered reusable investigation
  • a persistent investigation workspace
  • a source-linked briefing
  • a transparent source record
  • structured rate-limited JSON
  • a scoped component
  • a resilient degraded-mode artifact
  • A complete saved workspace
  • A portable export
  • A validated cross-product handoff receipt

Verification

  • Every CSV feature row has a corresponding saved result.
  • The JSON workspace ID and product version are preserved.
  • The export can be inspected without temporary browser state.
  • The receiving product identifies source, destination, schema, artifact, and review state.
  • A human reviewer can reproduce or audit the demonstration.

Responsible use

Public intelligence may be incomplete, delayed, or contested; do not use it for targeting, surveillance, emergency command, or unsupported prediction.

Replace synthetic data only with information you are authorized to use, after reassessing licensing, privacy, uncertainty, and review obligations.

articles.title: Site Intelligence: Troubleshooting and Recovery
articles.summary: Diagnose and recover common Site Intelligence failures without losing state.
articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
articles.prerequisites: Current product release; synthetic sample; no private data; human review
articles.content:
Site Intelligence · Troubleshooting

Connected country, regional, earth-systems, economic, humanitarian, trade, energy, event, alert, scenario, and briefing intelligence.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Last verified version: 3.0.0

Reading time: 18–30 minutes

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents
  • Purpose
  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This guide uses an evidence-first diagnostic sequence for Site Intelligence. Preserve state before changing configuration. Determine whether the failure comes from input data, permissions, version identity, browser assets, backend health, schema compatibility, external dependencies, or a product defect.

Prerequisites

  • Exact error message and time
  • Product, backend, package, and schema versions
  • Affected route, feature, record ID, and user role
  • A safe backup or export
  • The synthetic sample for a reference test

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Download CSV feature examples · Download JSON workspace example

Preview

#FeatureSample actionExpected resultClassification
1Country Dossiersopen a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadataa multi-domain country overviewsynthetic-demo
2Regional Comparisoncompare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Ugandaan aligned table or chartsynthetic-demo
3Earth Systems Observatoryreview rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signalsa time-bounded earth-systems viewsynthetic-demo
4Economics and Marketsinspect inflation, fiscal, food-price, and development indicatorsa sourced economic context viewsynthetic-demo
5Humanitarian and Conflictreview public displacement and humanitarian-need signalsa harm-aware summarysynthetic-demo

The downloadable files include all 16 feature rows.

Procedure

  1. Stop repeated imports, submissions, migrations, or destructive retries.
  2. Record URL, feature, action, role, versions, browser/runtime, and expected result.
  3. Run the same feature row in a new synthetic workspace.
  4. If the sample fails, inspect health, logs, browser console, network response, and validation details.
  5. If the sample succeeds, compare real input schema, units, permissions, size, source, and status with the sample.
  6. Check version and schema identity before clearing caches or reinstalling.
  7. Apply the smallest reversible change.
  8. Repeat the sample and real workflow, then document the result.

Common problems

SymptomLikely causeSafe response
Data unavailableprovider is delayed, rate-limited, or outside coveragecheck status, broaden period, or use a reviewed alternate
Version mismatchWordPress and backend versions differalign deployments and verify health endpoints
Misleading comparisondefinition, unit, geography, or period differscompare only compatible records
Live stream emptyfilters are too narrow or no event qualifiesexpand geography, time, or event type

Escalation package

  • Product and version
  • Feature and component
  • Exact error text
  • Reproduction steps using synthetic data
  • Expected and actual result
  • Redacted log excerpt without secrets or personal data
  • Whether a clean sample reproduces the problem

Expected output

  • A reproducible diagnosis or bounded unresolved issue
  • Preserved user and system state
  • A documented workaround when available
  • A support-ready issue summary without private data

Verification

  • The synthetic feature succeeds after the fix.
  • The original workflow succeeds or remains safely isolated.
  • Version and health identity are consistent.
  • No duplicates, stale locks, or unintended cache artifacts remain.
  • The resolution is reflected in a support article, known issue, or release record when appropriate.

Responsible use

Public intelligence may be incomplete, delayed, or contested; do not use it for targeting, surveillance, emergency command, or unsupported prediction.

Never place credentials, API keys, private URLs, personal records, client documents, or confidential logs in a public article or issue.

Site Intelligence · Technical Reference

Connected country, regional, earth-systems, economic, humanitarian, trade, energy, event, alert, scenario, and briefing intelligence.

Audience: product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators.

Status: Draft for product and editorial review.

Contents

  • Prerequisites
  • Sample data
  • Procedure
  • Expected output
  • Verification
  • Responsible use

Purpose

This reference summarizes the functional contract, record expectations, lifecycle, validation, export, compatibility, integration, privacy, and release-review requirements for Site Intelligence.

Prerequisites

  • Current release notes and repository documentation
  • A test environment
  • A backup and rollback plan
  • Access to product health or package checks
  • The synthetic all-feature sample

Sample data

This article uses a fictional Resilient Community Retrofit Program. The files contain one demonstration row for every documented feature, so each capability can be tested without private or production data.

Technical contract

Canonical identity

FieldValue
Product slugsite-intelligence
Product nameSite Intelligence
Verified version3.0.0
Content pack1.0.0
Default article statedraft
Human reviewrequired

Functional surface

CapabilitySample operationExpected artifact
Country Dossiersopen a Kenya dossier and inspect update and source metadataa multi-domain country overview
Regional Comparisoncompare compatible indicators for Kenya, Tanzania, and Ugandaan aligned table or chart
Earth Systems Observatoryreview rainfall anomaly, vegetation, weather, and hazard signalsa time-bounded earth-systems view

Core record fields

  • Stable record or artifact ID
  • Product and schema version
  • Created and updated time
  • Owner or responsible role
  • Lifecycle or review state
  • Source and provenance
  • Units, period, geography, classification, or audience where applicable
  • Validation findings and uncertainty
  • Privacy and publication boundary
  • Export or handoff integrity metadata

Lifecycle

Use a human-controlled lifecycle such as draft → review → approved/published, with revise, superseded, archived, withdrawn, failed, and recovered states where appropriate. Automated analysis may recommend; it must not silently approve, publish, or create private cases.

Validation

  • Required-field and type checks
  • Identifier and relationship checks
  • Unit, period, geography, method, and classification compatibility
  • Product and schema compatibility
  • Source, evidence, and provenance completeness
  • Permission, privacy, and publication-boundary checks
  • Deterministic checksum or fingerprint validation for portable artifacts

Integrations

  • Decision Studio
  • Research Librarian
  • Knowledge Library
  • Catalyst Data
  • Research Lab
  • Platform Core

Send the minimum required fields through a typed contract. Preserve source product, destination, schema, artifact ID, review state, provenance, consent or privacy boundary, and receipt. Private Contact and Engagement records never belong in public support or research artifacts.

Release review

  1. Confirm interface, service, manifest, package, and schema versions.
  2. Validate the all-feature sample.
  3. Run product tests and integrity checks.
  4. Test one export and each supported handoff category.
  5. Document breaking changes and migration path.
  6. Update Last Verified Version before publishing this article.

Expected output

  • A complete feature contract
  • A validated synthetic export
  • A compatibility decision
  • A documented integration receipt
  • A release-integrity report

Verification

  • Product slug, version, schema, and IDs agree.
  • Every documented feature maps to an actual interface, command, route, or record.
  • The sample files produce the stated artifacts.
  • Integrations preserve provenance and privacy.
  • Migration, rollback, support, and release documentation are current.

Responsible use

Public intelligence may be incomplete, delayed, or contested; do not use it for targeting, surveillance, emergency command, or unsupported prediction.

articles.audience: Product users, administrators, reviewers, and integrators
